description_long: This object is a karambit-style knife featuring a sharply curved, single-edged black blade with a pronounced point, ideal for slashing motions. The handle is ergonomically contoured and colored dark green, with finger grooves and a textured grip for secure handling. At the end of the handle, there is a prominent finger ring, a signature feature of karambit knives, allowing for advanced grip techniques and retention. The blade appears to be made of metal, likely steel, while the handle is constructed from a durable polymer or composite material. The knife lacks any visible guard or crossguard, and there are no decorative engravings or inlays present. The overall design is utilitarian, emphasizing function and grip security over ornamentation.
description: A curved, black-bladed karambit knife with a green, contoured handle and a finger ring at the end. The design is functional and grippy, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curved blade, green contoured handle, and finger ring.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the slim profile and the alignment of the blade and handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade curve, handle contours, and finger ring from the other side.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view emphasizing the thinness of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: contoured green polymer with finger grooves and a finger ring
